steps.title: Complete the contrast pair
steps.content: The passage says Saharan dust can have opposing effects on tropical cyclones. The first effect is introduced with On one hand and describes increased precipitation. The next sentence gives the opposite effect: lower sea surface temperatures that weaken the storm.

steps.title: Reject noncontrast links
steps.content: Choice B signals time, not opposition. Choice C introduces an example, but the sentence gives the other side of a contrast. Choice D signals a restatement, though weakening the storm is not a restatement of increasing precipitation. The needed transition completes the contrast pair. The answer is A.
